assign FC_data_add_INT_32      =  {  FC_INSTR_add_i      ,  UDMA_TX_add_i     ,  UDMA_RX_add_i     , DBG_RX_add_i     , FC_DATA_add_int    };
assign FC_data_wen_INT_32      =  {  FC_INSTR_wen_i      ,  UDMA_TX_wen_i     ,  UDMA_RX_wen_i     , DBG_RX_wen_i     , FC_DATA_wen_i      };
assign FC_data_wdata_INT_32    =  {  FC_INSTR_wdata_i    ,  UDMA_TX_wdata_i   ,  UDMA_RX_wdata_i   , DBG_RX_wdata_i   , FC_DATA_wdata_i    };
assign FC_data_be_INT_32       =  {  FC_INSTR_be_i       ,  UDMA_TX_be_i      ,  UDMA_RX_be_i      , DBG_RX_be_i      , FC_DATA_be_i       };
assign FC_data_aux_INT_32      =  {  FC_INSTR_aux_i      ,  UDMA_TX_aux_i     ,  UDMA_RX_aux_i     , DBG_RX_aux_i     , FC_DATA_aux_i      };
assign                            {  FC_INSTR_gnt_o      ,  UDMA_TX_gnt_o     ,  UDMA_RX_gnt_o     , DBG_RX_gnt_o     , FC_DATA_gnt_o      } = FC_data_gnt_INT_32     ;

assign                            {  FC_INSTR_r_opc_o    ,  UDMA_TX_r_opc_o   ,  UDMA_RX_r_opc_o   , DBG_RX_r_opc_o   , FC_DATA_r_opc_o    } = FC_data_r_opc_INT_32   ;

asset_candidates.label: APB peripheral register banks
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Registers can configure clocks, boot/fetch control, GPIO direction/output, timers/events, FLLs, SoC/cluster control, and other security-sensitive behavior.

asset_candidates.label: SoC control outputs
evidence_refs: apb_soc_ctrl ports fc_bootaddr_o, fc_fetchen_o, sel_hyper_axi_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_irq_o
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Controls processor/cluster boot address, fetch enable, reset, power, bypass, IRQ, and external interface selection.

asset_candidates.label: JTAG-visible SoC control register state
asset_candidates.security_relevance: JTAG/debug register paths may expose or modify SoC control state.

asset_candidates.label: FLL control/status interface
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Frequency/clock configuration can affect system operation; lock signals may mediate safe update/status.

asset_candidates.label: Clock divider configuration
evidence_refs: apb_clkdiv ports clk_div0/1/2 and valid outputs
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Software-controlled clock dividers can alter timing domains and peripheral operation.

asset_candidates.label: GPIO configuration and external pins
evidence_refs: apb_gpio ports gpio_in, gpio_in_sync, gpio_out, gpio_dir, interrupt
asset_candidates.security_relevance: GPIO direction and output configuration can expose or drive external signals.

asset_candidates.label: Timer and event generation state
evidence_refs: apb_timer_unit ports event_lo_i, event_hi_i, irq_lo_o, irq_hi_o, busy_o
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Timers/events/IRQs can trigger privileged flow or side effects.

asset_candidates.label: Memory regions and memory bus targets
asset_candidates.kind: ROM, L2/SCM memory, TCDM/local interconnect targets
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Memory regions are protected assets for read/write/fetch access; debug/JTAG/uDMA/fabric masters may reach them.

asset_candidates.label: uDMA subsystem and DMA transfer path
asset_candidates.security_relevance: DMA can read/write memory or peripherals and may require address/range mediation.

asset_candidates.label: MMU/sequence security sizing controls
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Memory-region sizing or security partition controls can define access-control boundaries.

asset_candidates.label: BIST/test controls and results
evidence_refs: pulp_interfaces ports bist_en, bist_pattern, bist_done_even/odd, bist_result_even/odd
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Test controls can override or observe memory/internal state if not mediated.

asset_candidates.label: Cache/private and flush control state
evidence_refs: pulp_interfaces ports ctrl_req_enable, ctrl_req_disable, flush_req, icache_is_private, ctrl_clear_regs, ctrl_enable_regs
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Cache enable/private-mode and flush state can affect isolation and state residue.

asset_candidates.label: Clock-gating and body-bias mux controls
asset_candidates.security_relevance: Power/timing controls may affect availability, test/debug modes, or isolation assumptions.

subject_candidates.label: APB bus requester/software-visible peripheral access path
Fabric/interconnect masters
evidence_refs: pulp_interfaces AXI-like and TCDM bus ports

guard_candidates.label: FLL lock/status mediation
guards: lock state may gate or qualify FLL update/status operations

guard_candidates.label: Debug/JTAG authorization candidate
guards: debug authorization/authentication or lifecycle state expected if debug reaches protected assets

guard_candidates.label: DMA/fabric address mediation candidate
guards: PMP/MMU/security partition check expected for protected memory

guard_candidates.label: Test/DFT mode mediation candidate
guards: test-mode enable authorization or lifecycle restriction expected

guard_candidates.label: Clock/power control validity guard
guards: valid/ack/lock sequencing and safe mode transition checks

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Reset initialization points
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Reset defaults of control registers and guard state should be checked for safe access and disabled debug/test defaults.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: APB transaction lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Read/write effects depend on valid protocol phase and address decode.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: FLL request/ack/lock lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Configuration changes may need to be blocked or sequenced based on lock/ack state.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Boot and fetch lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Boot/fetch controls determine execution start and cluster activation state.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Debug/JTAG visibility lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Debug-readable or writable state should be evaluated for authorization and persistence across reset/mode changes.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: DMA transfer lifecycle
states: uDMA TX/RX local interconnect request paths
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Configured transfers can remain active across policy/configuration updates if not synchronized.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Test/BIST lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Test operation may expose memory state or override normal access paths.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Cache control lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Transitions may leave stale private state or observable residue if not cleared/flushed.

state_lifecycle_facts.label: Clock/power lifecycle
state_lifecycle_facts.security_angle: Mode transitions can affect guard enforcement or availability if not sequenced.

path_candidates.label: APB requester to SoC control registers

summary: Permission-oriented semantic labeling of visible RTL scope: APB-accessible SoC peripherals, interconnects, debug/JTAG/test interfaces, memory buses, clock/reset/power controls, FLL/GPIO/timer configuration, uDMA and fabric paths are candidates for later vulnerability analysis. Labels are candidates only, not final vulnerability decisions.
